segments.text: Hello everyone. It is a real pleasure
being here today and I want to start by
thanking SonarQube for having us at this
great event. My name is Omer. I am the
head of partnerships at Tobaya software
engineering and developer productivity
insights platform and having with me
today is Emre Dundar the CPO and
co-founder of Aaya and Sukru Cakmak the
CTO and co-founder for as well and today
we will be discussing a very important
topic visibility and productivity
engineering in the age of AI. Starting
things off, I think we all can agree
that software development today is
moving faster than ever. We do deploy
more frequently, we automate more, we
measure more, we even integrate with
more tools than we ever did before. But
here is a simple question to start
things off. Are we actually more in
control than we were 5 years ago? We do
have more dashboards, more metrics, and
more reports. But are we making better
decisions?
Over the last decades, tools across the
SDLC has changed the way we perceive
things. Tools like SonarQube, for example,
transformed how we think about code
quality and the technical debt, for
example. That was a major leap forward.
We moved from manual inspection to
continuous quality visibility.
But as system became more disrupted,
something else happened. Complexity did
not actually disappear. It actually
multiplied.
And to understand where we're going next
in the age of AI, we need to understand
how we got there. And from here, I would
like to leave the mic to Emra.
Thank you, Amash. Hi, everyone. I'm
Emra, co-founder and chief product
officer at OA. I've spent over a decade
inside large enterprise engineering
organizations with thousands of
engineers, distributed teams, and
constant delivery pressure. And over
those years, one thing became clear,
very clear to me. Software
development didn't just improve, it
accelerated. I mean, just think about
it. We move from monoliths to
distributed systems, from manual QA and
deployments to automated CI/CD
processes, from waterfall to agile
devops platform engineering and now you
know wipe coding and powerful tools
emerge along the way. Tools tools
like Jira, CI/CD platforms, SonarQube
for managing quality security and
technical data.
especially in SonarCloud and Sonar

forget
in OA central position SonarQube is the
mandatory part because quality and
security is one of the biggest impact on
our software development life cycle.

fullText: Hello everyone. It is a real pleasure being here today and I want to start by thanking SonarQube for having us at this great event. My name is Omer. I am the head of partnerships at Tobaya software engineering and developer productivity insights platform and having with me today is Emre Dundar the CPO and co-founder of Aaya and Sukru Cakmak the CTO and co-founder for as well and today we will be discussing a very important topic visibility and productivity engineering in the age of AI. Starting things off, I think we all can agree that software development today is moving faster than ever. We do deploy more frequently, we automate more, we measure more, we even integrate with more tools than we ever did before. But here is a simple question to start things off. Are we actually more in control than we were 5 years ago? We do have more dashboards, more metrics, and more reports. But are we making better decisions? Over the last decades, tools across the SDLC has changed the way we perceive things. Tools like SonarQube, for example, transformed how we think about code quality and the technical debt, for example. That was a major leap forward. We moved from manual inspection to continuous quality visibility. But as system became more disrupted, something else happened. Complexity did not actually disappear. It actually multiplied. And to understand where we're going next in the age of AI, we need to understand how we got there. And from here, I would like to leave the mic to Emra. Thank you, Amash. Hi, everyone. I'm Emra, co-founder and chief product officer at OA. I've spent over a decade inside large enterprise engineering organizations with thousands of engineers, distributed teams, and constant delivery pressure. And over those years, one thing became clear, very clear to me. Software development didn't just improve, it accelerated. I mean, just think about it. We move from monoliths to distributed systems, from manual QA and deployments to automated CI/CD processes, from waterfall to agile devops platform engineering and now you know wipe coding and powerful tools emerge along the way. Tools tools like Jira, CI/CD platforms, SonarQube for managing quality security and technical data. especially in SonarCloud and Sonar servers, SonarQube servers. Don't forget in OA central position SonarQube is the mandatory part because quality and security is one of the biggest impact on our software development life cycle. Quality as I said earlier SonarQube is this is in the center of OA unit test
